mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating inflation signals overheating demand that forces monetary tightening expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both increase, or the interaction term shows zero marginal predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in industrial production indicate immediate supply chain disruptions that trigger short-term risk-off positioning, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction without corresponding macro data shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP growth confirm expanding economic activity that supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 0.4% signal loss of central bank price stability control, triggering equity sell-offs as discount rates rise, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.4%, or the threshold shows no predictive edge in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in total employment and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns when both conditions are met, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in housing starts signal strengthening consumer balance sheets and construction sector demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during recovery phases.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery periods, or the signal loses predictive power when housing inventory levels are not concurrently declining.
